name: Montañas Vacías Loop
name_original: Parque Natural del Alto Tajo – circular entre provincias de Guadalajara y Teruel
description: A difficult 47.9-mile mountain biking route in Alto Tajo, crossing Guadalajara and Teruel, with 3939 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Orea offers a diverse landscape characterized by the Montes Universales and the Alto Tajo Natural Park. The region features varied terrain, including river valleys like the Rio Guadalaviar, dense forests, and open mountain areas. Elevations range across the routes, providing a mix of climbs and descents for mountain bikers. Best mountain bike trails around Orea The most popular mountain bike trail is Birth of the Rio Guadalaviar — circular through the Montes Universales from Griegos, a 30.1 miles (48.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 8 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the headwaters of the Rio Guadalaviar within the Montes Universales. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Montañas Vacías Loop, a difficult 47.8 miles (77.0 km) path. This challenging loop traverses expansive mountain terrain, offering significant elevation changes. Local mountain bikers also love the Cerro Palillo - circular from Checa through the Alto Tajo Natural Park, a 23.6 miles (38.0 km) trail leading through the Alto Tajo Natural Park, often completed in about 3 hours 35 minutes. Mountain biking around Orea is defined by its mountain ranges, river systems, and protected natural parks. The network offers options for moderate to difficult ability levels, with 36 routes available. The routes in Orea are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 700 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Orea's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general difficulty level of mountain bike trails around Orea?
A: The mountain bike trails in Orea primarily cater to moderate to difficult skill levels. There are 24 moderate routes and 12 difficult routes available, but no easy trails are listed in this guide.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders in Orea?
A: Yes, Orea offers 12 difficult mountain bike trails for experienced riders. A notable challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004921016/montanas-vacias-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montañas Vacías Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 47.8 miles (77.0 km) long and features significant elevation changes.

Q: What are the typical lengths and durations of mountain bike trails in Orea?
A: Trail lengths vary, but many routes are substantial. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004953584/birth-of-the-rio-guadalaviar-circular-through-the-montes-universales-from-griegos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Birth of the Rio Guadalaviar — circular through the Montes Universales from Griegos&lt;/a&gt; is 30.1 miles (48.4 km) and takes around 4 hours 8 minutes, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004908762/cerro-palillo-circular-from-checa-through-the-alto-tajo-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Palillo - circular from Checa through the Alto Tajo Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; is 23.6 miles (38.0 km) and typically takes about 3 hours 35 minutes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Orea?
A: Orea's mountain bike trails traverse a diverse landscape, including river valleys like the Rio Guadalaviar, dense forests, and open mountain areas within the Montes Universales and Alto Tajo Natural Park. You can expect a mix of climbs and descents.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Orea?
A: The mountain bike trails in Orea are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 10 reviews. Over 700 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the natural beauty and challenging routes.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Orea?
A: Yes, many routes in Orea are circular. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004953584/birth-of-the-rio-guadalaviar-circular-through-the-montes-universales-from-griegos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Birth of the Rio Guadalaviar — circular through the Montes Universales from Griegos&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004908762/cerro-palillo-circular-from-checa-through-the-alto-tajo-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Palillo - circular from Checa through the Alto Tajo Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions to see near the mountain bike trails?
A: While riding, you might encounter natural and cultural highlights. Notable attractions include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2094395" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The towers of Chequilla&lt;/a&gt;, the charming settlements of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2530405" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orihuela del Tremedal&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5376107" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Griegos&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5167073" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Checa&lt;/a&gt;. You can also find refuges like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3924939" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Portera Refuge and Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: When is the best time of year for mountain biking in Orea?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Orea is typically during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be quite hot, and winter may bring snow, especially at higher elevations, making some trails less accessible or more challenging.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that pass through or near villages with amenities?
A: Many routes start from or pass near local villages. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31430859" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Griegos loop from Griegos&lt;/a&gt; begins in the village of Griegos, where you can find local amenities. Similarly, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004908762/cerro-palillo-circular-from-checa-through-the-alto-tajo-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Palillo - circular from Checa&lt;/a&gt; starts from Checa. Checking individual route details on komoot will provide specific information on nearby facilities.

Q: What should I know about wildlife when mountain biking in Orea?
A: Given Orea's location within the Montes Universales and Alto Tajo Natural Park, you are likely to encounter diverse wildlife. Riders should be mindful of their surroundings, respect natural habitats, and avoid disturbing animals. It's always a good idea to ride responsibly and keep noise to a minimum.

Q: Are there any particularly long mountain bike routes for endurance riders?
A: Yes, Orea offers several longer routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004921016/montanas-vacias-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montañas Vacías Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 47.8 miles (77.0 km) trail, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10418369" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lago El Cura – Orihuela del Tremedal loop from Orihuela del Tremedal&lt;/a&gt; covers 61.1 km, both suitable for endurance-focused riders.
tips: With chimney and wooden bunk beds, next to the lonely hermitage of San Lorenzo.

This shelter serves as a resting place and even as a place to spend the night during hikes. The surrounding area features stone tables and benches where you can sit and eat while enjoying the surroundings.
It is a beautiful place where the cattle move freely throughout the pasture. Without a doubt, a place where you can appreciate nature in all its splendor.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this circular mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route is considered difficult. It requires a very good physical condition and experience with mountain biking. You should be prepared for challenging sections, including unpaved and technically demanding terrain, and potentially needing to push your bike at times. The route involves significant elevation gain over its distance.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is predominantly mountainous and rugged, featuring a mix of dirt tracks, gravel paths, and marked trails. You'll encounter deep canyons carved into limestone and dolomite, with steep slopes and rocky cliffs. Wide tracks with considerable gradients are common, but narrow paths are also present. Be aware that after rainfall, certain areas can become tricky with mud and sticky clay.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for this 77 km route is approximately 6 hours and 47 minutes of riding time. However, given its 'difficult' rating and the challenging terrain, it's wise to allow for additional time for breaks, enjoying the scenery, and any unforeseen challenges.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: You can expect magnificent and diverse scenery. The route passes through dense pine, oak, and juniper forests. A major highlight is the crystal-clear, turquoise waters of the Tajo River, which snakes through long and deep canyons, forming impressive geological structures like knives, needles, and monoliths composed of limestone and red sandstone. The park's diverse ecosystems also support a rich array of flora and fauna.

faqs.question: Are there any specific points of interest or highlights on this route?
faqs.answer: Yes, along the route, you might encounter several points of interest. Notable highlights include the San Lorenzo Hermitage and Shelter, a Picnic area by the Tajo, the natural area of Dehesa de Boyales, and the charming settlement of Griegos.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: While the exact starting point and parking for this specific circular route aren't detailed, the Parque Natural del Alto Tajo generally has various access points. Given the route traverses both Guadalajara and Teruel provinces, you would typically look for parking in villages or designated areas within the park that align with your chosen starting point for the circular journey. It's advisable to check local information for specific trailhead parking.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this circular mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed in the Parque Natural del Alto Tajo, but they must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other visitors. As this is a natural park, always adhere to local regulations regarding pets, especially in sensitive ecological areas.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to mountain bike this route is typically during spring and autumn. These seasons offer more moderate temperatures, which are ideal for a challenging ride. Summers can be very hot, and winters may bring snow and ice, making some sections impassable or more difficult. Always check the weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to mountain bike in the Parque Natural del Alto Tajo?
faqs.answer: The Parque Natural del Alto Tajo does not typically require an entrance fee for general access. However, as a protected natural area and part of the Molina-Alto Tajo Geopark, specific activities or access to certain restricted zones might require permits. For mountain biking on established trails, a general permit is usually not needed, but it's always good practice to check the official park website for any updated regulations before your visit.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails in the Alto Tajo Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other well-known paths. Significant portions of the route follow sections of the MontañasVacías (58%) and the Camino Natural del Tajo (18%). You'll also find it briefly connects with trails like Rubiales - Orihuela del Tremedal (10%), [CIMA CU08] El Cubillo - El Portillo (4%), [CIMA TE12] El Portillo (4%), Camino de las Saguillas (6%), Camino de las Dehesas (4%), and Camino del Cerro de la Modorra (1%).

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and remote character, it's crucial to be well-prepared. You should bring plenty of water and snacks, appropriate mountain biking gear, a first-aid kit, and tools for bike repairs. Due to potential lack of mobile coverage, carrying a GPS device with offline maps and physical maps is highly recommended. Dress in layers suitable for varying mountain weather conditions.
